phase: consumed-development cross-asset stability and consensus-action screen
objective: Preserve Round 44 multi-horizon distributional forecast information while testing whether joint cross-asset learning and sharpness-aware optimization improve seed stability and whether epistemic-consensus medians produce enough fixed-cost actions for an economic screen.

diagnosis: All four horizons beat the unconditional quantile baseline by 2.33 to 4.31 percent and had positive pooled median-return rank association.
Central 80-percent and 50-percent interval coverage passed at every horizon.
The forecast gate failed only seed stability: four of twelve pair-horizon correlations were below 0.500, with a 0.452 minimum.
Every seed reached its best early-stop pinball loss at epoch 3 or 4 while training loss continued falling, indicating rapid overfit rather than optimization failure.
The lower-quartile-after-cost policy admitted one trade in nine months and cannot measure economic capacity.

research_basis.finding_used: Causal dilated temporal convolutions are a strong generic sequence-modeling baseline with long effective memory.
research_basis.limitation: The benchmark tasks are not financial returns and provide no trading-performance evidence.

research_basis.finding_used: Direct multi-horizon quantile forecasting and forking sequences can improve probabilistic forecast stability and sample efficiency.
research_basis.limitation: Demand and electricity forecasting do not establish crypto return predictability.

research_basis.finding_used: Cross-entity context can improve a shared multi-horizon probabilistic model; this motivates one joint BTC, ETH, and SOL temporal encoder.
research_basis.limitation: The reported retrieval results concern product demand and do not transfer as financial evidence.

research_basis.finding_used: Sharpness-aware minimization explicitly penalizes locally sharp solutions and can improve generalization under overparameterization.
research_basis.limitation: SAM approximately doubles gradient computation and its image benchmarks do not guarantee time-series improvement.

research_basis.finding_used: A recent multivariate time-series study found sharpness-aware optimization repaired rapid neural-forecast overfit; this supports a bounded SAM ablation.
research_basis.limitation: Its long-horizon benchmark data, transformer architecture, and MSE objective differ from this causal quantile-return task.

research_basis.finding_used: Disagreement among independently initialized models is epistemic uncertainty; the policy therefore requires worst-seed median consensus rather than hiding disagreement inside only an ensemble median.
research_basis.limitation: Deep-ensemble uncertainty is not a guarantee of calibrated financial tail risk.

data_contract.target_and_role_contract: Identical to the hash-bound Round 44 compounded hourly return, settled-funding, completeness, and role-boundary contract.
data_contract.derived_cache: Write one hash-manifested external NumPy cache of the verified Round 44-equivalent feature and target arrays for future diagnosis; no cache values may replace or alter the source identity.

chronological_roles.purpose: Architecture development only; no role can support promotion.

normalization_contract.feature_and_target_scalers: Bit-for-bit Round 44 mean, population-standard-deviation, clip, median, and scaled-MAD definitions.

candidates.optimizer: non-adaptive SAM over explicit non-foreach AdamW

model_contract.input_layout: Flatten the complete causal BTC, ETH, and SOL feature block at each hour into 213 channels; no future values and no cross-time attention.
model_contract.output_layout: Predict all three symbols jointly with 60 output channels: three symbols times four horizons times five monotone quantiles.
model_contract.quantile_parameterization: Per symbol and horizon, predict a median and four nonnegative softplus gaps so q10 <= q25 <= q50 <= q75 <= q90.
model_contract.loss: Equal-weight normalized pinball loss across three symbols, four horizons, and five quantiles.
model_contract.ensemble: Elementwise median of three independently initialized seed forecasts.
model_contract.candidate_selection: None; both candidates complete the same consumed-development diagnostics and gates.
model_contract.compute_backend: DirectML required for this host run; CPU remains a separately declared portability mode.

forecast_gate.gate_failure_action: Continue only to labeled descriptive consensus-policy diagnostics; deny AI action review, promotion, leverage, and untouched data.

optimizer_ablation_gate.claim_if_failed: SAM improvement is false; retain both raw candidate results without post-outcome changes.

descriptive_policy_contract.position_sleeves: Fixed one-third notional sleeve per symbol.
descriptive_policy_contract.entry_rule: When flat, evaluate every horizon. Long score is the minimum of the three seed median forecasts minus 12 bps round-trip cost. Short score is the negative maximum seed median forecast minus 12 bps. Enter only the side and horizon with the largest strictly positive score.
descriptive_policy_contract.rationale: Every seed must estimate positive after-cost median utility in the same direction; seed disagreement remains an explicit epistemic-risk veto while the return lower quartile is no longer misused as the expected-value forecast.
descriptive_policy_contract.holding_rule: Hold for exactly the selected 1, 4, 12, or 24-hour horizon, then flatten. No overlapping position is permitted within a symbol.
descriptive_policy_contract.stress_rule: Reprice the exact fixed base trade ledger; do not reselect horizons, sides, or timestamps under stress.

economic_gate.promotion_result: Still denied because the complete Round 45 evaluation is selection-contaminated development evidence.

ai_contract.future_ai_work: Improve the separate schema-constrained risk-review benchmark only after deterministic sequence-model execution; no language model may repair or reinterpret a failed numerical gate.

required_outputs: DirectML AdamW and SAM backward-update preflight and runtime evidence
per-candidate per-seed artifacts and exact reload checks
hashed seed and ensemble prediction tensors
per-candidate per-symbol per-horizon per-month forecast diagnostics
per-candidate seed stability and optimizer-ablation gate
per-candidate descriptive fixed consensus trade ledger
matched fixed-ledger base and stress economics
peak working set runtime and progress events
